ingredients.name: LIQUIDROOM
ingredients.address: LIQUIDROOM, 3 Chome-16-6 Higashi, Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0011, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: LIQUIDROOM in Ebisu appears in Season 2 as a live venue setting reflecting the competitive Tokyo live circuit that Roselia and rival bands navigate on their path to Budokan. Its real-world reputation as a stepping-stone venue for rising bands makes it an authentic backdrop for the band rivalry storyline.

ingredients.name: Soba Takashima
ingredients.address: Soba Takashima, 1 Chome-39-11 Sekiguchi, Bunkyo City, Tokyo 112-0014, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This soba shop near Sekiguchi appears in Episode 2 as a neighborhood eatery in the Bunkyo area, consistent with the daily-life scenes woven around Yukina and Lisa's home district. The quiet backstreet setting reflects the understated everyday moments the show places between intense band drama.

ingredients.name: JR Shinjuku East Exit Station Square
ingredients.localName: JR新宿駅 東口駅前広場
ingredients.address: 3 Chome-38-1 Shinjuku, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0022, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The busy Shinjuku East Exit plaza is depicted in Episode 1 as the bustling Tokyo transit hub where characters meet or pass through, establishing the show's urban Tokyo setting and the sense of scale the bands must conquer to reach Budokan.

ingredients.name: Shinjuku Expressway Bus Terminal
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒151-0051 Tokyo, Shibuya, 千駄ヶ谷５丁目２４, 55
ingredients.entityContext: Busta Shinjuku (the highway bus terminal) appears in Episode 3 as a departure point, used in a scene where a character travels to or from another city, underlining how the bands' ambitions extend beyond the immediate Tokyo neighborhood.

ingredients.name: Mejiro-dai 1-chome Playground
ingredients.localName: 文京区立目白台一丁目遊び場
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-16 Mejirodai, Bunkyo City, Tokyo 112-0015, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This small neighborhood playground in Bunkyo's Mejirodai district appears in Episode 9 as a quiet outdoor space near Yukina's home, used for a candid character moment between band members away from rehearsal pressure.

ingredients.name: Nippon Budokan
ingredients.address: 2-3 Kitanomarukoen, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 102-8321, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Nippon Budokan is the ultimate goal driving Roselia through the entire season. Their dream of performing here is declared in Episode 6, making this legendary arena the emotional center of BanG Dream! 2nd Season.

ingredients.name: Hachiko Memorial Statue
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-1 Dogenzaka, Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0043, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The iconic Hachiko statue at Shibuya Station is used in Episode 2 as a meeting point, a familiar Tokyo landmark that grounds the show's characters in recognizable real-world geography as they move between live venues.

ingredients.name: Otsukaekimae Station
ingredients.address: 3 Chome-33-1 Minamiotsuka, Toshima City, Tokyo 170-0005, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Otsuka Station area features in Episode 7 as part of the Toshima district surroundings, with the tram and station environment providing background for characters commuting across Tokyo in pursuit of rehearsal spaces and gigs.

ingredients.name: Gakushuin-Shita
ingredients.address: 7, Toshima City, Tokyo 171-0033, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Gakushuin-shita tram stop on the Toden Arakawa Line appears in Episode 1 as a neighborhood transit point near the Mejirodai district, placing fans at the exact street-level vantage point the show uses when following Yukina through her home area.

ingredients.name: Harumi-Futo
ingredients.address: Harumi-Futō, 5-chōme-8 Harumi, Chuo City, Tokyo 104-0053, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Harumi Pier in Chuo City appears in Episode 4 as a waterfront location used for a reflective scene, with the open Tokyo Bay backdrop providing visual contrast to the cramped studio interiors where the bands rehearse.

ingredients.name: Waseda Station
ingredients.address: Waseda Station, Wasedamachi, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 162-0042, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Waseda Station on the Toden Arakawa Line appears in Episode 7 as part of the Shinjuku-Bunkyo border commute route that characters use when traveling between their home neighborhood and rehearsal studios.

ingredients.name: Edogawabashi Jizo-dori Shopping Street
ingredients.address: Sugamo Jizodori Shopping Street, 4 Chome-22-8 Sugamo, Toshima City, Tokyo 170-0002, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This old-fashioned shotengai (shopping street) in Toshima/Sugamo appears in Episode 10 as a slice-of-life street setting, its retro character contrasting with the glamour of the live stages the bands aim for.

ingredients.name: Munatsuki-zaka Slope
ingredients.address: Munatsuki-zaka Slope, 1 Chome-1 Mejirodai, Bunkyo City, Tokyo 112-0014, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Munatsuki-zaka is the steep slope near Mejirodai that appears prominently in Episode 1. This hill runs directly through Roselia's home neighborhood and is one of the most photographed pilgrimage spots in the BanG Dream! franchise.

ingredients.name: Edogawa Park
ingredients.address: Edogawa Park, 2 Chome-1 Sekiguchi, Bunkyo City, Tokyo 112-8555, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Edogawa Park along the Kanda River in Bunkyo appears in Episode 3 as a relaxed outdoor setting where characters share a candid conversation, the riverside cherry-blossom scenery softening an otherwise tension-filled episode.

ingredients.name: NewDays KIOSK outside Shinjuku East Exit ticket gates
ingredients.localName: NewDays KIOSK 新宿東口改札外店
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒160-0022 Tokyo, Shinjuku City, Shinjuku, 3 Chome−38−1 JR 新宿駅 East Gate Ｂ１Ｆ NewDays KIOSK 新宿東口改札外店 外 B１左階段下
ingredients.entityContext: The NewDays kiosk just outside Shinjuku's East Exit gates appears in Episode 1 as a precise background detail confirming the show's meticulous recreation of the station area during a character transit scene.

ingredients.name: Komatsuka Bridge
ingredients.address: Komagata Bridge, 1 Chome-1 Higashikomagata, Sumida City, Tokyo 130-0005, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Komagata Bridge over the Sumida River appears in Episode 1 as a nighttime scenic shot, its illuminated arch reflected on the water providing a cinematic establishing image of Tokyo as the stage for the bands' ambitions.

ingredients.name: Asukayama Park
ingredients.address: Asukayama Park, 1 Chome-1-3 Oji, Kita City, Tokyo 114-0002, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Asukayama Park in Kita City appears in Episode 11 as a park outing location, its historic atmosphere and park-tram (the Asukayama Monorail) providing a distinctive background for a character bonding moment late in the season.

ingredients.name: Hozan Liquor Store
ingredients.entityContext: This small liquor shop in the Mejirodai area appears in Episode 9 as a neighborhood detail in Yukina's home district, one of the offgrid spots that pilgrims visit to recreate the show's hyper-local Bunkyo geography.

ingredients.name: Fujimizaka Slope
ingredients.entityContext: Fujimizaka Slope in Mejirodai appears in Episode 9 near Yukina's home territory, another of the residential hill roads in this quiet Bunkyo neighborhood that the show renders in close detail.

ingredients.name: Toshima Ward Minamiotsuka Park
ingredients.address: Minami-Otsuka Park, 2 Chome-27-1 Minamiotsuka, Toshima City, Tokyo 170-0005, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Minami-Otsuka Park near Otsuka Station appears in Episode 7 as a local park setting during a scene where characters interact in the Toshima district, bridging transit scenes around the Toden Arakawa tram line.

ingredients.name: Kusurino Fukutaro Otsuka Ekimae Shop
ingredients.localName: くすりの福太郎大塚駅前店
ingredients.address: Kusurino Fukutaro Otsuka Ekimae Shop, 1 Chome-16-5 Kitaotsuka, Toshima City, Tokyo 170-0004, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This drugstore near Otsuka Station appears in Episode 7 as a background detail in the Toshima shopping district, one of the street-level real-world locations that BanG Dream! uses to authenticate its Tokyo neighborhood settings.

ingredients.name: Otsuka Ekimae Station
ingredients.address: Otsukaekimae Station, 3-chōme-33 Minamiōtsuka, Toshima City, Tokyo 170-0005, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Otsuka Ekimae Station on the Toden Arakawa tram line appears in Episode 7 as a boarding point used by characters commuting through the Toshima area, its vintage tram character matching the show's affection for the older parts of northern Tokyo.

ingredients.name: Tsurumaki Minami Park
ingredients.address: Tsurumaki Minami Park, ７８ Wasedatsurumakicho, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 162-0041
ingredients.entityContext: Tsurumaki Minami Park near Waseda appears in Episode 6 as a small neighborhood park in the Shinjuku/Bunkyo border zone, seen briefly during a character's walk through the local streets around the Waseda area.

ingredients.name: Sound Studio NOAH Sangenjaya
ingredients.localName: サウンドスタジオノア 三軒茶屋店
ingredients.address: Sound Studio NOAH Sangenjaya, 1 Chome-5-15 Sangenjaya, Setagaya City, Tokyo 154-0024, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Sound Studio NOAH in Sangenjaya is depicted in Episode 5 as a rehearsal studio where one of the bands practices, capturing the unglamorous but essential grind of rehearsal that defines the show's portrayal of band life.

ingredients.name: Toyohashi
ingredients.address: Toyohashi, Aichi, Japan

ingredients.name: Sound Studio NOAH Komazawa
ingredients.localName: サウンドスタジオノア 駒沢店
ingredients.address: Sound Studio NOAH Komazawa, 4 Chome-2-7 Kamiuma, Setagaya City, Tokyo 154-0011, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Sound Studio NOAH Komazawa appears in Episode 5 alongside the Sangenjaya branch, reflecting how multiple bands in Season 2 use the Sound Studio NOAH chain across Tokyo for intensive pre-live rehearsals.

ingredients.name: Sangenjaya Station
ingredients.address: Sangenjaya Station, 2 Chome-15 Taishido, Setagaya City, Tokyo 154-0004, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Sangenjaya Station on the Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line appears in Episode 5 as the transit hub serving the Sound Studio NOAH rehearsal branch in that area, placing fans at the exact station entrance the characters use when arriving for band practice.

ingredients.name: Ecolo Park SYFORME GAKUSHUIN SHITA Parking Lot
ingredients.localName: エコロパーク SYFORME GAKUSHUINSHITA駐車場
ingredients.address: Ecolo Park SYFORME GAKUSHUINSHITA Parking Lot, 2 Chome-10-12 Takada, Toshima City, Tokyo 171-0033, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This parking lot near Gakushuin-shita appears in Episode 4 as a street-level background detail in the Mejirodai district, one of the hyper-specific real-world landmarks that BanG Dream! pilgrimage guides use to map Yukina's home neighborhood.

ingredients.name: Nanzoin Temple
ingredients.address: Nanzo-in Temple, 1035 Sasaguri, Kasuya District, Fukuoka 811-2405, Japan

ingredients.name: Kamo Brother Music Center
ingredients.localName: 加茂ブラザーミュージックセンター
ingredients.address: Kamo Brother Music Center, 3528-7 Otacho, Minokamo, Gifu 505-0041, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kamo Brother Music Center in Minokamo, Gifu appears in Episode 3 as a music instrument shop connected to one character's provincial background, referenced as the kind of local music store where a band member first encountered her instrument.

ingredients.name: Eisei Bunko Museum
ingredients.address: Eisei Bunko Museum, 1 Chome-1-1 Mejirodai, Bunkyo City, Tokyo 112-0015, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Eisei Bunko Museum in Mejirodai appears in Episode 3 as a background landmark in Yukina's home neighborhood. Its stately Edo-period villa grounds are visible in the Mejirodai streetscape that the show reproduces closely.
